title: OIE Community Store manifest (oie.json)
description: Per-release manifest that describes an extension to the OIE Community Store. Lives at the repository root and is read at each release tag, so its values are version-specific.

id.description: Stable extension id. MUST equal the 'path' attribute of the extension's plugin.xml. Used to match store records against the engine's installed inventory for update detection.

name.description: Human-readable display name shown in the store.

description.description: One or two sentences describing what the extension does.

type.description: Extension category. connector, plugin, and datatype are installable through the store today; code-template-library and channel are listed but not yet installable.

version.description: Extension version. MUST equal the release tag, modulo an optional leading 'v' (tag v1.4.0 -> version 1.4.0). Semantic-versioning comparable, so the store can detect updates.

minEngineVersion.description: Lowest engine version this release supports (inclusive). Omit or null for no lower bound.

maxEngineVersion.description: Highest engine version this release supports (inclusive). Omit or null for no upper bound.

filename.description: Name of the release asset to install. Optional: if omitted, the store uses the single .zip asset on the release. Supports a {version} placeholder, e.g. 'sqs-connector-{version}.zip'.

restartRequired.description: Whether activating the extension needs an engine restart. Defaults to true for installable (binary) types.

homepage.description: Project homepage. Defaults to the GitHub repository URL.

documentation.description: External documentation URL, surfaced as a 'Documentation' button.

storeDocs.description: Repo-relative path to the markdown rendered in the store detail view. Optional; if omitted the store looks for store.md, docs/store.md, then README.md. Must be a relative path (no leading '/', no '..').

license.description: SPDX license identifier, e.g. MPL-2.0 or Apache-2.0.

deprecated.description: Marks the extension as deprecated; the store shows a Deprecated badge.

deprecationMessage.description: Optional explanation shown when deprecated is true (e.g. a replacement to use instead).
